Title: The plastidial pentose phosphate pathway is essential for postglobular embryo development in Arabidopsis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic phosphate translocator family; xylulose-5-phosphate/phosphate translocator
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in embryo are higher than or comparable to GPT1 from preglobular to torpedo stages, though lower in most seed tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels in embryo are higher than or comparable to GPT1 from preglobular to torpedo stages
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential import route for pentose phosphates into plastid
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of two insertion mutants and genetic combinations with tkl1 and pgl3
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of XPT alone, or together with tkl1 or pgl3 background, does not alter the requirement for oxidative plastidial OPPP during early embryo development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xpt null mutants had no detectable developmental or seed phenotype. 2. xpt did not enhance pgl3 globular arrest and did not shift tkl1 arrest earlier than torpedo stage. 3. Therefore pentose phosphate import via XPT is not required for development through the globular stage.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotype as single mutant under study conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Exports newly synthesized purines from plastid to cytosol for nucleic acid synthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of bt1 T-DNA insertion mutant, seed segregation, embryo microscopy
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of BT1 causes globular-stage arrest with raspberry-like embryos and seed abortion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bt1 siliques contained about 25% white seeds. 2. Mutant embryos arrested at the globular stage with raspberry-like morphology similar to pgl3 and gpt1. 3. This shows export of plastid-synthesized adenine nucleotides is essential for embryo morphogenesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports interpretation that purine demand is for nucleic acid synthesis in cytosol rather than only plastidial cytokinin synthesis

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for production of ribulose-5-phosphate precursor pool for ribose-5-phosphate synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports purine nucleotide and nucleic acid synthesis required for embryo and endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid and peroxisome; embryo lethality attributable to loss of plastidial activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of independent T-DNA mutant alleles pgl3-2 and pgl3-3; seed segregation; embryo microscopy; comparison with hypomorphic pgl3-1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of plastidial PGL3 causes embryo arrest at the globular stage with raspberry-like embryos and seed abortion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Null pgl3 alleles produced about 25% white aborted seeds. 2. Mutant embryos arrested at the globular stage and showed abnormal protoderm cell enlargement and raspberry-like morphology. 3. A partial-loss allele pgl3-1 was viable, indicating an essential dosage requirement for plastidial OPPP flux.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key essential step for embryonic plastidial OPPP flux; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The lethal phenotype is due to loss of plastidial, not peroxisomal, activity; data argue against toxicity of accumulated lactones as primary cause
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates 6-phosphogluconate to ribulose-5-phosphate with NADPH production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nucleotide and nucleic acid synthesis required for embryo morphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in seed tissues during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed tissues during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant identification, transcript analysis, genetic crosses with pgd3 mutants, seed segregation, embryo microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single pgd1 loss alone is normal, but combined loss with PGD3 abolishes plastidial PGD activity and causes embryo abortion at or before the globular stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pgd1 single mutants were viable and normal. 2. Complete plastidial PGD deficiency required combined loss of PGD1 and PGD3 and caused embryo lethality. 3. The double-deficient embryos arrested from preglobular to globular stages with raspberry-like morphology, supporting necessity of full oxidative OPPP flux.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential redundant step together with PGD3; not individually rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with PGD3 is embryo/gametophyte lethal
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PGD1 and PGD3 form homo- and heterodimers; plastidial activity is mainly attributable to these two isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates 6-phosphogluconate to ribulose-5-phosphate with NADPH production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nucleotide and nucleic acid synthesis required for embryo morphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in seed tissues during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed tissues during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant identification, transcript analysis, genetic crosses with pgd1 mutants, seed segregation, embryo microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single pgd3 loss alone is normal, but combined loss with PGD1 causes embryo abortion at or before the globular stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pgd3 single mutants were viable and normal. 2. Combined plastidial PGD deficiency with pgd1 caused early embryo abortion and some gametophytic lethality. 3. Arrested embryos ranged from preglobular to globular stages and often showed raspberry-like morphology.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential redundant step together with PGD1; not individually rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with PGD1 is embryo/gametophyte lethal
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual PGD1 activity in pgd1-2 background allowed slightly later arrest, supporting dosage dependence
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastidial oxidative/nonoxidative pentose phosphate interface and purine precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-connecting step immediately downstream of oxidative OPPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target ribose-5-phosphate required for purine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for purine nucleotide and nucleic acid synthesis during early embryo and endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in embryo proper and syncytial endosperm at preglobular stage; increases in all seed tissues during development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in embryo proper and syncytial endosperm at preglobular stage; increases in all seed tissues during development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of two embryo-lethal insertion alleles, segregation analysis, embryo microscopy, developmental staging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of plastidial RPI3 causes preglobular embryo arrest, abnormal division planes, defective endosperm proliferation, and seed abortion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rpi3 mutants failed to develop beyond octant/preglobular stages. 2. Embryos displayed abnormal cell division orientation from the earliest divisions, indicating defective pattern formation. 3. Results show plastidial conversion of Ru5P to R5P is essential for early embryo viability and endosperm development.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential terminal determinant for plastidial R5P formation in early embryos
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RPI4 is unlikely to compensate because of low early embryo expression and probable catalytic divergence
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies alternative nonoxidative intermediate rather than direct target R5P
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports downstream nonoxidative OPPP flux and E4P generation, but not required for early globular progression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of two insertion mutants, segregation analysis, embryo staging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RPE3 is lethal but embryos progress beyond the globular stage to at least torpedo stage, indicating Xyl5P production is not required for early embryo development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Homozygous rpe3 mutants were absent, indicating lethality. 2. Mutant embryos reached at least torpedo stage before arrest. 3. Therefore RPE3-dependent Xyl5P generation is dispensable for development through the globular stage.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established for target phenotype; not essential for early globular progression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete loss is lethal later in development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to distinguish requirement for R5P versus Xyl5P production in early embryos

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Transketolase reactions generating Xyl5P and erythrose-4-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a two-carbon unit between sugar phosphates, enabling formation of xylulose-5-phosphate and erythrose-4-phosphate from fructose-6-phosphate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies alternative intermediate pools and E4P branch rather than direct R5P requirement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: aromatic amino acid precursor pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports later embryo development and aromatic precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed throughout embryo development; major embryo TKL isoform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed throughout embryo development; major embryo TKL isoform
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Two independent lethal insertion mutants, segregation analysis, embryo microscopy, xpt double mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TKL1 does not block development through the globular stage but causes delayed embryos that arrest at torpedo stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tkl1 mutants were embryo lethal with about 25% white seeds. 2. Mutant embryos progressed beyond globular stage and arrested at torpedo stage. 3. xpt double mutants showed the same timing, demonstrating that TKL1-dependent bypass is not required for early globular progression.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established for early R5P-dependent phenotype; important for later development
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete loss is embryo lethal at later stage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates that E4P/Xyl5P-producing bypasses are insufficient and unnecessary for early embryo progression through globular stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed transcript datasets used for contextual expression analysis (Belmonte et al. 2013); accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphoribosyl-formylglycinamidine and glycine to 5-aminoimidazole ribonucleotide (AIR)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream committed purine biosynthetic step downstream of R5P/PRPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of purine nucleotides from ribose-5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for nucleic acid synthesis and early embryo/endosperm proliferation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Two independent T-DNA insertion lines, segregation analysis, embryo microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PUR5 causes very early embryo arrest at the 1- to 2-cell stage and severe endosperm defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pur5 mutants were embryo lethal. 2. Mutant embryos arrested by 2 DAF and remained at 1-cell or 2-cell stages by 4 DAF. 3. This supports the conclusion that purine synthesis downstream of R5P is essential for the earliest stages of embryo development.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential downstream purine synthesis step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AIR is also a precursor for thiamine biosynthesis, but paper interprets embryo arrest primarily through purine/nucleic acid deficiency

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms chorismate, precursor of aromatic amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports aromatic amino acid and derived metabolite biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Embryo-lethal insertion line analysis, segregation analysis, embryo microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of chorismate synthase causes embryo arrest at torpedo stage, showing chorismate synthesis is not required for morphogenesis through the globular stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cs-1 was embryo lethal. 2. Mutant embryos arrested at torpedo stage rather than preglobular/globular stage. 3. Therefore the early requirement for plastidial OPPP is not due to providing E4P for chorismate synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete loss is embryo lethal later in development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a discriminating pathway test rather than a central mechanistic focus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts orotate to OMP and then decarboxylates OMP to UMP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly supports pyrimidine nucleotide and nucleic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for nucleic acid synthesis during early embryo and endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion line, segregation analysis, embryo microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of UMPS causes embryo arrest at the zygote/1-cell stage with severe endosperm defects, demonstrating pyrimidine synthesis is essential for early development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Homozygous umps mutants were absent and transmission was reduced. 2. Embryos in abnormal seeds arrested at elongated zygote or 1-cell stage. 3. These data support nucleic acid deficiency as a primary cause of early arrest, not only purine deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential step in pyrimidine synthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete loss is embryo lethal and may reduce transmission
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pathway is independent of plastidial purine pathway and not required for cytokinin synthesis, strengthening nucleic-acid interpretation
